title: Career Agency, Success Criteria, and Search Constraints
description: A graduate is told to apply everywhere immediately, but has not defined acceptable work, financial runway, access needs, location, schedule, authorization, values, safety, or a stop rule. Learners cumulatively produce learner-owned career decision charter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define a personally meaningful career outcome with observable success, guardrails, tradeoffs, and a review date.
Separate hard constraints, flexible preferences, assumptions, unknowns, and resources without disclosing private reasons unnecessarily.
Model time, money, energy, care, health, and learning capacity under conservative search scenarios and escalation thresholds.
